_comment: Knob cap bake — single neutral white plastic dome rendered under the same studio rig as chassis.json. Runtime tints per-knob with `core_color` via painter.image()'s color multiply, so this one bake serves every section colour (yellow/red/green/blue/grey/master).

canvas._comment: Render at 256² native; runtime scales down to the actual core_radius (typically 9–11 px). Higher native gives clean specular falloff at any GUI scale (1×/1.5×/2×).

cap._comment: A slightly convex disk: flat top with rounded bevel at the rim. Higher height + bigger bevel gives the dome more curvature → more obvious key-light gradient and a real rim highlight from the upper-right rim light.

material._comment: Bright neutral base so runtime tints multiply *down* without losing highlight detail. Egui painter.image() multiplies texture × tint_color, so a bake with peak whites at ~240/255 lets a tint like (212,149,38) reach near-saturation in lit areas. Slightly lower roughness (0.42) so the key light produces a visible specular sheen on the dome — still matte plastic, not glossy.

lights._comment: Same key/fill/rim directions as chassis.json so the cap reads as lit by the identical studio rig. Energies pushed harder so the dome curvature reads — peak white at upper-left ≈ 250/255, bottom-right shadow ≈ 110/255, top-right rim a thin bright band.

render._comment: scale=4 → 1024×1024 native. The visible knob in-GUI is ~32 px diameter at 1× UI / ~64 px at 2× UI; baking at 1024 gives ≥16× supersampling so the dome's lighting falloff stays smooth at any GUI scale (1×/1.5×/2×) with linear-filter texture sampling.
